steps.title: Identify the joined clauses
steps.content: The quoted Nobel criterion forms one complete idea, and the words after the blank begin another complete idea: 'in 1912, for instance, judges recognized Gerhart Johann Robert Hauptmann.' The second clause gives an example of the first, so the sentence needs punctuation that can join two independent clauses.

steps.title: Reject weaker punctuation
steps.content: Choice A, 'direction"', gives no punctuation between the clauses. Choice B, 'direction" and,', adds an awkward coordinating conjunction with misplaced punctuation. Choice D, 'direction,"', uses only a comma, which cannot join two independent clauses by itself.

steps.title: Place the semicolon
steps.content: Choice C, 'direction";', closes the quotation and places a semicolon after the closing quotation mark, correctly joining the two independent clauses. The answer is C.
